mnemonics.title: PUE Formula
mnemonics.content: PUE = Total Facility Energy ÷ IT Equipment Energy. Remember: 'Put Useful Energy' - the useful energy divided by total.

mnemonics.title: Green IT Pyramid Layers
mnemonics.content: Hardware → Virtualization → Cloud → Software → Metrics. Remember: 'Her Vicar Says More' - Her (Hardware), Vicar (Virtualization), Says (Cloud), More (Metrics)

acronyms.usage: Data center energy efficiency metric - lower is better, ideal is 1.0
Complete cost analysis including acquisition, operation, and disposal
Financial benefit measurement for green IT initiatives
Cloud model where software is delivered over the internet
Cloud model providing virtualized computing resources
Cloud model providing development platforms and tools

visualTips.visualization: Think of PUE as a pie chart: If total energy is the whole pie (1.0) and IT equipment is the shaded portion, PUE shows how much goes to cooling, lighting, etc. The smaller the non-IT portion, the better the PUE.
Picture a triangle with broad base (Hardware) narrowing to top (Metrics). Each level builds on previous - can't do cloud without virtualization, can't measure without software optimization.
Think of virtualization like a apartment building - one physical building (server) contains multiple independent units (virtual machines), each with own resources but sharing common infrastructure.

analogies.analogy: Like a bus that takes multiple passengers (virtual machines) to different destinations, instead of running multiple smaller vehicles (servers). More efficient use of the 'engine' (hardware).
Like electricity - you pay for what you use, don't build your own power plant. Cloud provides computing 'power on demand' without owning infrastructure.
Like a dumb terminal vs a personal computer. The 'brain' is in the server, the client just displays results - much simpler and energy-efficient hardware.
